stem: A food manufacturing study measures the weight (grams) for a sample of 30 cereal boxes. The sample mean is 259.5 g with a sample standard deviation of 53.0 g. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 259.5 g. 95% CI = [239.7, 279.3]. Point estimate: 259.5 g.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
